description:
Route `cmd_init` through `Store::init_xdg` so production `bl init` writes the XDG layout. By this point every integration test is layout-tolerant; the flip flips green.

## Scope

- `src/commands/init.rs`: replace `Store::init(&cwd, stealth, tasks_dir)` with `Store::init_xdg(&cwd, stealth, tasks_dir)` on the non-`--bare` path. Update the module-doc.
- Verify the four §14.x gates from Phase 1B-2 still pass (they should — they hit `init_xdg` directly, not `cmd_init`).
- Add the rest of §14.19: a full lifecycle (init → create → claim → review → close) on a post-flip clone leaves `git log main` with exactly the one `[bl-xxxx]` delivery commit and nothing else. Half of this gate landed in Phase 1B-2; this slice closes it.
- Run the full `scripts/pre-commit` gate (clippy + line lengths + tests + tarpaulin 100% coverage). The pre-commit hook will fail the commit if any test or the coverage check fails — this is the load-bearing gate for the slice.

## Why it can land alone

By dependency, Phase 1B-1 through 1B-4 have already moved every assertion site off legacy paths. The flip is then a one-function-call change.

## Dependency

Hard depends on Phase 1B-4 (and transitively on 1B-1 through 1B-3). Without those the test suite breaks the moment `cmd_init` changes.

## Out of scope

- `bl --bare` XDG-awareness — sibling Phase 1B-7.
- `bl remaster` XDG-awareness — sibling Phase 1B-7.
- EffectiveConfig migration of `load_config` call sites — sibling Phase 1B-6.

## Conformance

Closes §14.19 (full lifecycle no-on-main-writes). The other gates (§14.1, §14.4, §14.15) already pass from Phase 1B-2.
title: Phase 1B-5: flip cmd_init to Store::init_xdg, close §14.19 lifecycle gate
